criteria.label: State Var(ŷ) in words and symbols for the bias–variance decomposition.
criteria.example: Write Var(ŷ(x)) = E_D[(ŷ(x) − E_D[ŷ(x)])²] and explain it as prediction instability when retraining on different samples from the same population.

criteria.label: Diagnose high variance from train vs validation metrics.
criteria.example: Given train accuracy 98% and validation 76%, label the situation high variance and note the large gap versus a 58%/57% pair which would suggest high bias.

criteria.label: Write the squared-error decomposition and name all three components.
criteria.example: E[(y − ŷ)²] = Bias²(ŷ) + Var(ŷ) + Var(ε|x); identify which term is irreducible noise.

criteria.label: Interpret a learning curve that shows validation improving with training size while training stays high.
criteria.example: Conclude variance is likely limiting generalization and recommend collecting more data before adding model capacity.

criteria.label: Contrast Var(ŷ) with Var(X) and point to the correct prerequisite topic.
criteria.example: Explain Var(X) spreads outcomes of X; Var(ŷ) spreads predictions across training sets; cite Expectation & Variance for Var(X).

criteria.label: Propose two variance-reduction tactics for an overfit decision tree.
criteria.example: Suggest max_depth=6, min_samples_leaf=10, or a random forest; mention the bias increase if depth is cut too aggressively.
